questions.question: A cylindrical water tank has a radius of 4 feet and a height of 12 feet. What is the volume of the tank in cubic feet? (Use π ≈ 3.14.)
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = 3.14 × 4² × 12 = 3.14 × 16 × 12 = 3.14 × 192 = 602.88 cubic feet.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 3 cm and a height of 8 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters? (Use π ≈ 3.14.)

A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 inches by 6 inches by 10 inches. A smaller rectangular prism with dimensions 2 inches by 3 inches by 5 inches is removed from it. What is the volume of the remaining solid in cubic inches?
questions.explanation: Volume of large prism = 5 × 6 × 10 = 300 cubic inches. Volume of small prism = 2 × 3 × 5 = 30 cubic inches. Remaining volume = 300 − 30 = 270 cubic inches.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 5 inches. What is its surface area in square inches? (Use π ≈ 3.14.)
questions.explanation: Surface area of a sphere = 4πr² = 4 × 3.14 × 5² = 4 × 3.14 × 25 = 314 square inches.

questions.question: Two identical cylinders are stacked vertically. Each cylinder has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 6 cm. What is the total volume of both cylinders combined? (Use π ≈ 3.14.)
questions.explanation: Volume of one cylinder = πr²h = 3.14 × 3² × 6 = 3.14 × 9 × 6 = 169.56 cm³. Total for two cylinders = 2 × 169.56 = 339.12 cm³.

questions.question: A cone and a cylinder have the same radius of 4 inches and the same height of 9 inches. How many times larger is the volume of the cylinder than the volume of the cone?
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π(4²)(9). Volume of cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π(4²)(9). The ratio is πr²h ÷ [(1/3)πr²h] = 3.
